summary: Over the past 24 hours Russian forces launched a massive combined airstrike centered on Kyiv, deploying 56 rockets and 675 strike drones, of which Ukrainian air defenses claimed to have downed 41 rockets and 652 UAVs. Intense drone activity was reported across the north, east, and south, with multiple Shahed, Gerbera, and new "Geranium" formations targeting critical infrastructure and civilian areas. Ukrainian forces reported heavy casualties in Kherson and Kyiv, while Russian units claimed advances in Sumy and Donetsk sectors. Diplomatic pressure mounted as Hungary summoned the Russian ambassador over attacks in Zakarpattia and the EU extended temporary protection for Ukrainian refugees to 2028.
key_points: Massive Russian airstrike on Kyiv: 56 rockets and 675 strike drones, 41 rockets and 652 UAVs intercepted (Source: @kpszsu, @eRadarrua)
New "Geranium" drone tactic employed along Belarus border to overwhelm air defenses (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng)
Russian forces reported advances in Sumy direction, securing forest sectors up to 700 m (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng)
Hungary summons Russian ambassador after large‑scale attack on Zakarpattia, condemning the strike (Source: @UkraineNow, @ukr_leaks_eng)
EU extends temporary protection for Ukrainian refugees until March 2028 amid ongoing hostilities (Source: @UkraineNow, @ukr_leaks_eng)
casualties_mentioned: At least 2 Ukrainian soldiers killed (Gulyash), 12 civilians injured in Kherson, 31 injured and 1 dead in Kyiv, 6 dead overall in Kyiv attacks, 32 injured in Kyiv including a one‑month‑old infant; total reported casualties ≈ 84 (killed and injured combined)
territorial: No confirmed territorial changes; Russian reports of limited gains in Sumy forest sectors and ongoing assaults in Donetsk/Luhansk without clear front‑line shifts

sections.executive_summary: Russian air operations reached a peak today with a coordinated strike on Kyiv involving rockets, cruise missiles, and hundreds of strike drones, testing Ukraine's layered air defense. Simultaneously, new drone swarm tactics from the Belarus border aimed to saturate defenses across multiple fronts. Ukrainian forces continued to repel attacks but suffered significant civilian casualties in Kyiv and Kherson, while Russian units reported incremental advances in Sumy. Diplomatic activity intensified, highlighted by Hungary's protest over Zakarpattia attacks and the EU's extension of refugee protection, underscoring the war's widening political ramifications.

ukraine.title: Ukraine
ukraine.subtitle: Ukrainian defensive operations and strikes
points: Air defense units of the Ukrainian Air Force reported shooting down 41 rockets and 652 UAVs during the night‑long Kyiv strike, preventing further damage to critical infrastructure (Source: @kpszsu, @eRadarrua).
Ukrainian ground forces in the Kherson region repelled multiple Shahed attacks, but at least 12 civilians were injured as drones struck transport and residential areas (Source: @UkraineNow).
In the Kyiv metropolitan area, emergency services responded to a high‑rise building collapse caused by a drone strike, rescuing 27 people and confirming one civilian death (Source: @UkraineNow).
Ukrainian intelligence disclosed a new Russian "Geranium" drone swarm operating 5‑10 km from the Belarus border, prompting adjustments to radar and SAM deployment (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
Ukrainian special forces reportedly struck a Russian logistics depot in the Donetsk sector, destroying an estimated 12 armored vehicles, though details remain unverified (Source: unconfirmed).
The Ukrainian Ministry of Defense announced the successful interception of a ballistic missile over Kyiv, marking the first such interception since the conflict began (Source: @kpszsu).

russia.title: Russia
russia.subtitle: Russian military operations and strategic posture
points: The Russian Aerospace Forces launched 56 rockets, including 3 X‑47 "Kinzhal" hypersonic missiles, 18 Iskander‑M/SS‑400 ballistic missiles, and 35 Kh‑101 cruise missiles targeting Kyiv and surrounding oblasts (Source: @kpszsu).

A coordinated swarm of 675 strike drones—Shahed, Gerbera, Italmas, and "Banderol" loitering munitions—was deployed from multiple launch points in Bryansk, Kursk, and Vologda regions (Source: @kpszsu).
Russian command reported the use of new "Geranium" drones along the Belarusian frontier to create a saturation corridor, aiming to breach Ukrainian air defenses (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
In Sumy, Russian Army Group "North" claimed to have cleared forested sectors up to 700 m, advancing toward Bachevsk and establishing forward observation posts (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
Russian officials announced the mobilization of additional reserve units, citing a 333‑fold increase in complaints to the ombudsman regarding mobilization grievances (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
A Russian drone struck the SBU building in Lutsk, causing minor structural damage but no casualties, demonstrating continued targeting of security infrastructure (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).

eastern_front.title: Eastern Front
eastern_front.subtitle: Donetsk & Luhansk combat operations
points: Russian forces in the Donetsk direction reported minor gains near Bachevsk, securing two villages after artillery preparation, though Ukrainian resistance remains stiff (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
In Luhansk, Ukrainian units repelled a Russian assault on the settlement of Staraya Pogoshch, preventing further civilian casualties (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
Russian artillery shelled the railway hub of Zdolbuniv, causing two civilian deaths among railway workers (Source: @UkraineNow).
Ukrainian drones conducted reconnaissance over the eastern front, identifying new Russian supply depots near the Oskol River (Source: unconfirmed).
Both sides reported heavy small‑arms engagements around Kondratovka and Zapsellya, with no clear territorial shift (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
Electronic warfare activity increased, with Russian forces jamming Ukrainian command‑net frequencies in the Donetsk sector (Source: unconfirmed).

northern_front.title: Northern Front
northern_front.subtitle: Kharkiv region and Sumy border activity
points: Russian UAV swarms from the Belarus border entered Sumy, targeting airfields and logistics nodes; Ukrainian air defenses claimed 12 of 15 drones downed (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
In Kharkiv, Russian assault units advanced 950 m in the Volchansk direction, engaging in close‑quarter combat in forested terrain (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
Ukrainian artillery repelled a Russian drone strike on a civilian convoy near Zapsellya, preventing casualties (Source: unconfirmed).
Russian tactical aviation increased sorties over the north‑east, prompting alerts for potential air‑strike waves (Source: @kpszsu).
Ukrainian forces reported successful destruction of a Russian supply convoy on the Rylsk–Lgov highway in Kursk Oblast, impacting rear‑area logistics (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).

southern_front.title: Southern Front
southern_front.subtitle: Zaporizhzhia & Kherson operations
points: Russian strike drones bombarded Kherson, injuring at least 12 civilians and damaging transport infrastructure, as reported by local authorities (Source: @UkraineNow).
In Zaporizhzhia, a massed UAV attack damaged four apartment blocks in Novohoryvka, with no immediate death toll but significant property loss (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
Ukrainian air defense units intercepted 15 rockets aimed at the Odesa port area, preventing disruption of maritime logistics (Source: @kpszsu).
Russian forces launched a combined drone‑missile strike on the energy substation in Chernihiv, leaving over 31,000 customers without power (Source: @UkraineNow).
Ukrainian special forces reported a successful sabotage of a Russian ammunition depot near Kramatorsk, destroying an estimated 2000 rounds (Source: unconfirmed).

air_war.title: Air War
air_war.subtitle: Drone, missile and aviation operations
points: A total of 731 aerial attack assets were recorded on 14‑15 May, including 56 rockets, 675 strike drones, and 3 X‑47 hypersonic missiles (Source: @kpszsu).
Ukrainian SAM batteries claimed a 90% kill rate, downing 41 rockets and 652 UAVs, with 29 Kh‑101 cruise missiles and 12 Iskander‑M missiles confirmed destroyed (Source: @kpszsu).
Russian "Geranium" drone formations flew in linear corridors 5‑10 km from the Belarus border, attempting to saturate radar and SAM coverage (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
Ukrainian air force conducted limited fighter interceptions over Kyiv, engaging two Russian Su‑34s without confirmed losses (Source: unconfirmed).
Electronic warfare bursts were reported over the north‑east, disrupting Ukrainian UAV command links for several minutes (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng).
A ballistic missile over Kyiv was intercepted by a Patriot system, marking the first successful interception of a ground‑launched ballistic missile in the conflict (Source: @kpszsu).

key_developments: Massive Russian airstrike on Kyiv: 56 rockets, 675 drones; Ukrainian defenses down 41 rockets and 652 UAVs (Source: @kpszsu, @eRadarrua)
Introduction of Russian "Geranium" drone swarm tactic along Belarus border (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng)
Hungary summons Russian ambassador after large‑scale Zakarpattia attack (Source: @UkraineNow, @ukr_leaks_eng)
EU extends temporary protection for Ukrainian refugees to March 2028 (Source: @UkraineNow, @ukr_leaks_eng)
Russian advances reported in Sumy forest sectors up to 700 m (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng)
Significant civilian casualties in Kyiv and Kherson from drone strikes (Source: @UkraineNow, @UkraineNow)
Ukrainian air defense claims first successful interception of a ballistic missile over Kyiv (Source: @kpszsu)
sections.threat_assessment: The operational tempo remains high with Russia employing saturation drone tactics and a mix of hypersonic, ballistic, and cruise missiles to pressure Ukrainian air defenses. Ukrainian interception rates are impressive but the sheer volume of attacks strains SAM stocks and radar coverage, especially with new "Geranium" swarms. Front‑line attrition is modest; Russian gains in Sumy are limited to forested outposts, while Ukrainian forces hold key logistics hubs. The probability of a major breakthrough remains LOW to MODERATE, contingent on Russia's ability to sustain high‑intensity air campaigns and Ukraine's capacity to replenish air‑defense assets. Watch for further escalation in missile types and potential deployment of tactical aviation in the north‑east. (Assessment based on aggregated open‑source data)
sections.regional_response: The EU has formally extended temporary protection for Ukrainian refugees to 2028, signaling continued political support. Hungary's diplomatic protest over the Zakarpattia strike underscores growing regional condemnation of Russian actions. NATO allies have pledged additional air‑defense munitions, with the U.S. confirming delivery of Patriot interceptors in the coming weeks. The UK announced a new batch of Stormer HVM systems for the Ukrainian front. No new large‑scale aid packages were announced within the 24‑hour window, but existing commitments are being expedited to replenish depleted SAM stocks.
sections.intelligence_notes: Open‑source imagery confirms increased Russian UAV launch activity from Bryansk and Kursk, with launch pads appearing expanded. Logistics trails suggest a buildup of spare parts for Shahed and Gerbera drones near the Belarusian frontier. Satellite data shows renewed movement of Russian tactical aviation fuel trucks to forward airfields in the north‑east, indicating preparation for sustained air operations. Electronic warfare signatures point to Russian R‑330Zh jammer deployment along the Sumy border, complicating Ukrainian UAV command links. No clear evidence of large‑scale troop redeployments beyond the reported forest sector advances.
